rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use visible feature names in component type contracts, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths
Declare AGREE types as int, real, or bool; avoid 'integer' or 'boolean'
Do not reference AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions; use exposed ports or constants instead
Align helper declaration types with expression types (e.g., bool for comparisons, int for integer arithmetic)
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Avoid implementation-only paths in component type contracts
Declare AGREE types correctly (int, real, bool)
Do not reference AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Declare constants with `const name : type = value;`
Declare equations with `eq name : type = expression;`
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not reference AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions
Do not use 'integer' or 'boolean' as AGREE type names
Do not align helper declaration types incorrectly with expression types
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
